rationale: Title Y is stronger overall because it gives HR readers the ablation modality detail, population, study size, and the clinically relevant renal decline signal. It is longer than ideal, but the added specificity improves its editorial value.
loser_weakness: Title X is cleaner, but too generic. It omits the pentaspline platform, sample size, and renal decline finding, so it reads more like a broad mechanistic comparison than a specific prospective study.
